hook: CBC World and The Diplomat — opposite political universes — both said the same thing about Strait of Hormuz / US-Iran deal. Nobody else did.

tweets.text: 33 buckets, 59 articles, 5 distinct frames in today's coverage.
Frame: ECONOMIC_BLOCKADE_EFFICACY (1 bucket). "How the U.S. blockade is starting to hurt Iran's economy" — CBC World
Frame: CEASEFIRE_FRAGILITY (1 bucket). "U.S. says it intercepted Iranian attacks on 3 navy ships in Strait of Hormuz" — CBC World

Frame: REGIONAL_ECONOMIC_CRISIS (1 bucket). "The economic fallout from the war in the Middle East has dominated talks between senior Southeast Asian officials at the 48th ASEAN Summit…" — The Diplomat
Frame: DEAL_NEGOTIATION_WINDOW (1 bucket). "Trump recua e diz que operação para reabrir Hormuz será suspensa em tentativa de finalizar acordo" — Folha de São Paulo
The paradox: US blockade intent and ASEAN consequence diverge but converge on strait closure as power instrument. (CBC World & The Diplomat, opposite blocs.)
**Brazil - Folha de São Paulo** (brazil): Trump pauses operations pending 30-day negotiations.
